revision.comment: 新規： 鹿児島県にあるクスノキの巨木
revision.text:
[[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[蒲生町]]の[[蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の巨木である。
==概要== 高さ約30メートル、幹の周囲24.2メートル（地上から1.3メートル）、根回り33.5メートルあり、1988年（昭和63年）に行われた環境庁巨樹巨木調査において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。幹の中には広さ約13平方メートルの空洞があり、樹齢は約1500年と推定されている。
==歴史== [[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡を創建した1123年（保安4年）から神木とされており、当時すでに相当の大木であったと考えられている。1506年（永正3年正月7日）に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。1913年（大正2年）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱とされ、1922年（大正11年）3月8日には天然記念物に、1952年（昭和27年）3月29日には[[特別天然記念物]]に指定されている。1985年（昭和60年）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。
==参考文献== *蒲生町郷土誌編さん委員会編 『蒲生町郷土誌』 蒲生町、1991年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かもうのくす}} [[Category:蒲生町]] [[Category:著名な木]] [[Category:特別天然記念物]] [[Category:植物天然記念物]]

{{coor title dms|31|45|56.04|N|130|34|10.64|E}} [[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[蒲生町]]の[[蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の巨木である。
==概要== 高さ約30メートル、幹の周囲24.2メートル（地上から1.3メートル）、根回り33.5メートルあり、1988年（昭和63年）に行われた環境庁巨樹巨木調査において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。幹の中には広さ約13平方メートルの空洞があり、樹齢は約1500年と推定されている。
==歴史== [[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡を創建した1123年（保安4年）から神木とされており、当時すでに相当の大木であったと考えられている。1506年（永正3年正月7日）に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。1913年（大正2年）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱とされ、1922年（大正11年）3月8日には天然記念物に、1952年（昭和27年）3月29日には[[特別天然記念物]]に指定されている。1985年（昭和60年）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。
==参考文献== *蒲生町郷土誌編さん委員会編 『蒲生町郷土誌』 蒲生町、1991年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かもうのくす}} [[Category:蒲生町]] [[Category:著名な木]] [[Category:特別天然記念物]] [[Category:植物天然記念物]]

{{coor title dms|31|45|56.04|N|130|34|10.64|E}} [[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[姶良市]][[蒲生町]]の[[蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の巨木である。

==歴史== [[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡を創建した1123年（保安4年）から神木とされており、当時すでに相当の大木であったと考えられている。1506年（永正3年正月7日）に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。1913年（大正2年）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱とされ、1922年（大正11年）3月8日には天然記念物に、1952年（昭和27年）3月29日には[[特別天然記念物]]に指定されている。1985年（昭和60年）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。

==概要== 高さ約30メートル、幹の周囲24.2メートル（地上から1.3メートル）、根回り33.5メートルあり、1988年（昭和63年）に行われた環境庁巨樹巨木調査において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。幹の中には広さ約13平方メートルの空洞があり、樹齢は約1500年と推定されている。
==歴史== [[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡を創建した1123年（保安4年）から神木とされており、当時すでに相当の大木であったと考えられている。1506年（永正3年正月7日）に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。1913年（大正2年）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱とされ、1922年（大正11年）3月8日には天然記念物に、1952年（昭和27年）3月29日には[[特別天然記念物]]に指定されている。1985年（昭和60年）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。

revision.comment: [[蒲生八幡神社]]の2010年10月2日 14:33 (UTC) から一部転記 (主執筆者： 鳩神人)
revision.text:
{{coor title dms|31|45|56.04|N|130|34|10.64|E}} [[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[姶良市]][[蒲生町 (鹿児島県)|蒲生町]]の[[蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の巨木である。
==概要== 高さ約30メートル、幹の周囲24.2メートル（地上から1.3メートル）、根回り33.5メートルあり、1988年（昭和63年）に行われた環境庁巨樹巨木調査において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。幹の中には広さ約13平方メートルの空洞があり、樹齢は約1500年と推定されている。
==歴史== [[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡を創建した1123年（保安4年）から神木とされており、当時すでに相当の大木であったと考えられている。1506年（永正3年正月7日）に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。1913年（大正2年）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱とされ、1922年（大正11年）3月8日には天然記念物に、1952年（昭和27年）3月29日には[[特別天然記念物]]に指定されている。1985年（昭和60年）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。
==参考文献== *蒲生町郷土誌編さん委員会編 『蒲生町郷土誌』 蒲生町、1991年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かもうのくす}} [[Category:姶良市]] [[Category:著名な木]] [[Category:特別天然記念物]] [[Category:植物天然記念物]]
高さ30メートル、目通し幹囲24.2メートル、根回り33.6メートルの[[楠|大楠]]で、内部に[[畳]]8畳分の空洞がある。推定樹齢は1,600年（平成22年現在）。大正11年（[[1922年]]）3月8日に国の[[天然記念物]]に指定、昭和27年（[[1952年]]）3月29日には[[特別天然記念物]]とされ、昭和63年（[[1988年]]）度の[[環境庁]]による巨樹・巨木林調査で日本一の巨樹であると認定された。[[宇佐八幡宮神託事件]]で大隅へ配流された[[和気清麻呂]]が当地を訪れ、手にしていた杖を地に刺したものが根付いたものと伝えられ（[[杖立て伝説]]）、神社創祀時には既に[[神木]]として祀られていたといい、また[[出水市]]の市指定天然記念物である「出水の大楠」とは相思の仲で、この両樹に纏わる悲恋物語も伝えられている<ref>[http://powerdesign.bbplus.net/0_Kamou80000/KamouSite/oukusu/oukusu.html 蒲生八幡神社、「日本一の巨木」]（平成22年10月2日閲覧）。</ref>。

{{coor title dms|31|45|56.04|N|130|34|10.64|E}} [[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[姶良市]][[蒲生町 (鹿児島県)|蒲生町]]の[[蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の巨木である。国の[[特別天然記念物]]。
== 概要 == 高さ約30メートル、地上から1.3メートルの高さでの幹周は24.2メートルで、根回り33.5メートル、幹の中には広さ約13平方メートル（[[畳]]8畳分）の空洞があり、樹齢は約1600年と推定されている（[[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）現在）。[[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に行われた[[環境庁]]による巨樹巨木調査（昭和63年度）において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。
==歴史== [[宇佐八幡宮神託事件]]で大隅へ配流された[[和気清麻呂]]が当地を訪れ、手にしていた杖を地に刺したものが根付いたものと伝えられ（[[杖立て伝説]]）、[[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡神社を創建した[[保安 (元号)|保安]]4年（[[1123年]]）の時点で既に[[神木]]として祀られていたともされているので<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://powerdesign.bbplus.net/0_Kamou80000/KamouSite/oukusu/oukusu.html 蒲生八幡神社、「日本一の巨木」]（平成22年10月2日閲覧）。</ref>、当時から相当の大木であったと考えられている。[[永正]]3年（[[1506年]]）正月7日に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。
[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱<!--西では？-->とされ、同11年3月8日には[[天然記念物]]に、昭和27年（[[1952年]]）3月29日には特別天然記念物に指定されている。昭和60年（[[1985年]]）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。
なお、同県[[出水市]]の市指定天然記念物である「出水の大楠」とは相思の仲で、この両樹に纏わる悲恋物語も伝えられている<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *蒲生町郷土誌編さん委員会編 『蒲生町郷土誌』 蒲生町、1991年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かもうのくす}} [[Category:姶良市]] [[Category:著名な木]] [[Category:特別天然記念物]] [[Category:植物天然記念物]]

{{coor title dms|31|45|56.04|N|130|34|10.64|E}} [[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[姶良市]][[蒲生町 (鹿児島県)|蒲生町]]上久徳の[[蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の巨木である。国の[[特別天然記念物]]。
== 概要 == 高さ約30メートル、地上から1.3メートルの高さでの幹周は24.2メートルで、根回り33.5メートル、幹の中には広さ約13平方メートル（[[畳]]8畳分）の空洞があり、樹齢は約1600年と推定されている（[[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）現在）。[[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に行われた[[環境庁]]による巨樹巨木調査（昭和63年度）において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。
==歴史== [[宇佐八幡宮神託事件]]で大隅へ配流された[[和気清麻呂]]が当地を訪れ、手にしていた杖を地に刺したものが根付いたものと伝えられ（[[杖立て伝説]]）、[[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡神社を創建した[[保安 (元号)|保安]]4年（[[1123年]]）の時点で既に[[神木]]として祀られていたともされているので<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://powerdesign.bbplus.net/0_Kamou80000/KamouSite/oukusu/oukusu.html 蒲生八幡神社、「日本一の巨木」]（平成22年10月2日閲覧）。</ref>、当時から相当の大木であったと考えられている。[[永正]]3年（[[1506年]]）正月7日に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。
[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱<!--西では？-->とされ、同11年3月8日には[[天然記念物]]に、昭和27年（[[1952年]]）3月29日には特別天然記念物に指定されている。昭和60年（[[1985年]]）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。
なお、同県[[出水市]]の市指定天然記念物である「出水の大楠」とは相思の仲で、この両樹に纏わる悲恋物語も伝えられている<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>。

== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *蒲生町郷土誌編さん委員会編 『蒲生町郷土誌』 蒲生町、1991年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かもうのくす}} [[Category:姶良市]] [[Category:著名な木]] [[Category:特別天然記念物]] [[Category:植物天然記念物]]

{{coor title dms|31|45|56.04|N|130|34|10.64|E}} [[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[姶良市]][[蒲生町 (鹿児島県)|蒲生町]]上久徳の[[蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の[[巨樹|巨木]]である。国の[[特別天然記念物]]。
== 概要 == 高さ約30メートル、地上から1.3メートルの高さでの幹周は24.2メートルで、根回り33.5メートル、幹の中には広さ約13平方メートル（[[畳]]8畳分）の空洞があり、樹齢は約1500年と推定されている（[[平成]]22年（[[2010年]]）現在）。[[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に行われた[[環境庁]]による巨樹巨木調査（昭和63年度）において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。
==歴史== [[宇佐八幡宮神託事件]]で大隅へ配流された[[和気清麻呂]]が当地を訪れ、手にしていた杖を地に刺したものが根付いたものと伝えられ（[[杖立て伝説]]）、[[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡神社を創建した[[保安 (元号)|保安]]4年（[[1123年]]）の時点で既に[[神木]]として祀られていたともされているので<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://powerdesign.bbplus.net/0_Kamou80000/KamouSite/oukusu/oukusu.html 蒲生八幡神社、「日本一の巨木」]（平成22年10月2日閲覧）。</ref>、当時から相当の大木であったと考えられている。[[永正]]3年（[[1506年]]）正月7日に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。
[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱<!--西では？-->とされ、同11年3月8日には[[天然記念物]]に、昭和27年（[[1952年]]）3月29日には特別天然記念物に指定されている。昭和60年（[[1985年]]）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。
なお、同県[[出水市]]の市指定天然記念物である「出水の大楠」とは相思の仲で、この両樹に纏わる悲恋物語も伝えられている<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>。

[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱<!--西では？-->とされ、同11年3月8日には[[天然記念物]]に、昭和27年（[[1952年]]）3月29日には特別天然記念物に指定されている。昭和60年（[[1985年]]）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */ +{{国指定文化財等データベース}}
revision.text:
[[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[姶良市]][[蒲生町 (鹿児島県)|蒲生町]]上久徳の[[蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の[[巨樹|巨木]]である。国の[[特別天然記念物]]。
== 概要 == 高さ約30メートル、地上から1.3メートルの高さでの幹周は24.2メートルで、根回り33.5メートル、幹の中には広さ約13平方メートル（[[畳]]8畳分）の空洞があり、樹齢は約1500年と推定されている（[[平成]]23年（[[2011年]]）現在）。[[昭和]]63年（[[1988年]]）に行われた[[環境庁]]による巨樹巨木調査（昭和63年度）において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。蒲生の大楠・蒲生の大クスという表記もみられる。
==歴史== [[宇佐八幡宮神託事件]]で大隅へ配流された[[和気清麻呂]]が当地を訪れ、手にしていた杖を地に刺したものが根付いたものと伝えられ（[[杖立て伝説]]）、[[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡神社を創建した[[保安 (元号)|保安]]4年（[[1123年]]）の時点で既に[[神木]]として祀られていたともされているので<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://powerdesign.bbplus.net/0_Kamou80000/KamouSite/oukusu/oukusu.html 蒲生八幡神社、「日本一の巨木」]（平成22年10月2日閲覧）。</ref>、当時から相当の大木であったと考えられている。[[永正]]3年（[[1506年]]）正月7日に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。
[[大正]]2年（[[1913年]]）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱<!--西では？-->とされ、同11年3月8日には[[天然記念物]]に、昭和27年（[[1952年]]）3月29日には特別天然記念物に指定されている。昭和60年（[[1985年]]）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。
なお、同県[[出水市]]の市指定天然記念物である「出水の大楠」とは相思の仲で、この両樹に纏わる悲恋物語も伝えられている<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *蒲生町郷土誌編さん委員会編 『蒲生町郷土誌』 蒲生町、1991年
==外部リンク== * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2978|蒲生のクス}} * [http://www.kagoshima-kankou.com/guide/11083/ 蒲生の大楠 日本一の巨樹] - 鹿児島県観光連盟 * [https://www.jataff.jp/monument/48.html 蒲生のクス] - 農林水産・食品産業技術振興協会
{{coord|31|45|56.75|N|130|34|9.99|E|region:JP-46|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもうのくす}} [[Category:姶良市の歴史]] [[Category:著名なクス]] [[Category:特別天然記念物]] [[Category:植物天然記念物]]

==歴史== [[宇佐八幡宮神託事件]]で大隅へ配流された[[和気清麻呂]]が当地を訪れ、手にしていた杖を地に刺したものが根付いたものと伝えられ（[[杖立て伝説]]）、[[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡神社を創建した[[保安 (元号)|保安]]4年（[[1123年]]）の時点で既に[[神木]]として祀られていたともされているので<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html 蒲生八幡神社、「日本一の巨木」]（2021年10月16日閲覧）。</ref>、当時から相当の大木であったと考えられている。[[永正]]3年（[[1506年]]）正月7日に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。

なお、同県[[出水市]]の市指定天然記念物である「出水の大楠」とは相思の仲で、この両樹に纏わる悲恋物語も伝えられている<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>。

[[Image:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|蒲生のクス]] '''蒲生のクス'''（かもうのクス）は、[[鹿児島県]][[姶良市]][[蒲生町 (鹿児島県)|蒲生町]]上久徳の[[蒲生八幡神社 (姶良市)|蒲生八幡神社]]境内にある[[クスノキ]]の[[巨樹|巨木]]である。国の[[特別天然記念物]]。
== Overview == {{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=蒲生のクスの位置|places= {{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''蒲生のクス'''|mark=Red pog.svg}} {{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[鹿児島市]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}} }} 高さ約30メートル、地上から1.3メートルの高さでの幹周は24.2メートルで、根回り33.5メートル、幹の中には広さ約13平方メートル（[[畳]]8畳分）の空洞があり、樹齢は約1500年と推定されている（[[平成]]23年（[[2011]]）現在）。[[昭和]]63年（[[1988]]）に行われた[[環境庁]]による巨樹巨木調査（昭和63年度）において日本最大の巨木と認定されている。蒲生の大楠・蒲生の大クスという表記もみられる。
==歴史== [[宇佐八幡宮神託事件]]で大隅へ配流された[[和気清麻呂]]が当地を訪れ、手にしていた杖を地に刺したものが根付いたものと伝えられ（[[杖立て伝説]]）、[[蒲生舜清]]が蒲生八幡神社を創建した[[保安 (元号)|保安]]4年（[[1123]]）の時点で既に[[神木]]として祀られていたともされているので<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html 蒲生八幡神社、「日本一の巨木」]（2021年10月16日閲覧）。</ref>、当時から相当の大木であったと考えられている。[[永正]]3年（[[1506]]）正月7日に八幡の森で発生した火災が延焼し枝の一部を焼失した。
[[大正]]2年（[[1913]]）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱<!--西では？-->とされ、同11年3月8日には[[天然記念物]]に、昭和27年（[[1952]]）3月29日には特別天然記念物に指定されている。昭和60年（[[1985]]）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。
なお、同県[[出水市]]の市指定天然記念物である「出水の大楠」とは相思の仲で、この両樹に纏わる悲恋物語も伝えられている<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
==参考文献== *蒲生町郷土誌編さん委員会編 『蒲生町郷土誌』 蒲生町、1991年
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat}} * {{国指定文化財等データベース|401|2978|蒲生のクス}} * [https://www.kagoshima-kankou.com/guide/11083 蒲生の大楠 日本一の巨樹] - 鹿児島県観光連盟 * [https://www.jataff.jp/monument/48.html 蒲生のクス] - 農林水産・食品産業技術振興協会

[[大正]]2年（[[1913]]）に発表された「大日本老樹番付」で東の横綱<!--西では？-->とされ、同11年3月8日には[[天然記念物]]に、昭和27年（[[1952]]）3月29日には特別天然記念物に指定されている。昭和60年（[[1985]]）8月31日、[[昭和60年台風第13号|台風13号]]の強風のため多くの枝葉を失ったが町民の保護活動によって復活している。

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamō no Kusu]][[ja:蒲生のクス]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621332]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11621332]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:00, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox Japanese Temple Tree
| 名称 = {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|qq=|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}
| image = [[File:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|{{ill|Kamo Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}]]
| 所在地 = [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]][[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]][[:en:Kamō, Kagoshima|Kamō Town]]
| 地図 = 
| 緯度度 = 
| 経度度 = 
}}

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant [[:en:Camphora officinarum|kusunoki]] tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|qq=|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 23 ([[:en:2011|2011]])). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ([[:en:1988|1988]]) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|qq=|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* Kamō Town Local History Magazine Committee, ''Kamō Town Local History'', Kamō Town, 1991

== External Links ==

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant [[:en:Camphora officinarum|kusunoki]] tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|qq=|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 23 ([[:en:2011|2011]])). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ([[:en:1988|1988]]) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|qq=|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

| 地図 = 
| 緯度度 =

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant [[:en:Camphora officinarum|kusunoki]] tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 23 ([[:en:2011|2011]])). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ([[:en:1988|1988]]) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

{{Infobox Japanese Temple Tree
| 名称 = {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}
| image = [[File:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|{{ill|Kamo Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}]]
| 所在地 = [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]][[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]][[:en:Kamō, Kagoshima|Kamō Town]]
| 地図 = 
| 緯度度 = 
| 経度度 = 
}}

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant [[:en:Camphora officinarum|kusunoki]] tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 23 ([[:en:2011|2011]])). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ([[:en:1988|1988]]) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant [[:en:Camphora officinarum|kusunoki]] tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 23 ([[:en:2011|2011]])). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ([[:en:1988|1988]]) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

{{Infobox Japanese Temple Tree
| 名称 = {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}
| image = [[File:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|{{ill|Kamo Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}]]
| 所在地 = [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]][[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]][[:en:Kamō, Kagoshima|Kamō Town]]
| 地図 = 
| 緯度度 = 
| 経度度 = 
}}

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant [[:en:Camphora officinarum|kusunoki]] tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 23 ([[:en:2011|2011]])). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ([[:en:1988|1988]]) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

| image = [[File:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|{{ill|Kamo Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}]]

| 緯度度 =

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant [[:en:Camphora officinarum|kusunoki]] tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 23 ([[:en:2011|2011]])). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ([[:en:1988|1988]]) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant [[:en:Camphora officinarum|kusunoki]] tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in [[:en:Aira, Kagoshima|Aira City]], [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]]. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>[[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]]</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 23 ([[:en:2011|2011]])). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ([[:en:1988|1988]]) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, [[:en:Wake no Kiyomaro|Waki Kiyomaro]]—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the [[:en:Hōan|Hōan era]] (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ([[:en:Shinboku|Shinboku]]), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the [[:en:Eishō (Muromachi period)|Eishō Era]] (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ([[:en:1913|Taisho 1]]), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a [[:en:Natural monument|Natural Monument]], and later, on March 29, 1952 ([[:en:1952|Showa 27]]), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in [[:en:Izumi, Kagoshima|Izumi City]], which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=kusunoki}} tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|en|Aira, Kagoshima|WD=Q407947|lt=Aira City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>{{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City}}</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 23 ({{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}})). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ({{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988}}) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Waki Kiyomaro}}—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the {{ill|Hōan|en|Hōan|WD=Q1194464|lt=Hōan era}} (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ({{ill|Shinboku|en|Shinboku|WD=Q48743901|lt=Shinboku}}), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Eishō (Muromachi period)|en|Eishō (Muromachi period)|WD=Q1193577|lt=Eishō Era}} (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ({{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}}), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument}}, and later, on March 29, 1952 ({{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in {{ill|Izumi, Kagoshima|en|Izumi, Kagoshima|WD=Q15734|lt=Izumi City}}, which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=kusunoki}} tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|en|Aira, Kagoshima|WD=Q407947|lt=Aira City}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>{{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City}}</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 23 ({{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011}})). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ({{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988}}) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Waki Kiyomaro}}—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the {{ill|Hōan|en|Hōan|WD=Q1194464|lt=Hōan era}} (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ({{ill|Shinboku|en|Shinboku|WD=Q48743901|lt=Shinboku}}), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Eishō (Muromachi period)|en|Eishō (Muromachi period)|WD=Q1193577|lt=Eishō Era}} (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ({{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}}), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument}}, and later, on March 29, 1952 ({{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in {{ill|Izumi, Kagoshima|en|Izumi, Kagoshima|WD=Q15734|lt=Izumi City}}, which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

{{Infobox Japanese Temple Tree
| 名称 = {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}
| image = [[File:Big Japanese cinnamon Kamo hachiman shrine.jpg|thumb|right|300px|{{ill|Kamo Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Hachiman Shrine|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}]]
| 所在地 = {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}{{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|en|Aira, Kagoshima|WD=Q407947|lt=Aira City|1=Aira, Kagoshima}}{{ill|Kamō, Kagoshima|en|Kamō, Kagoshima|WD=Q1044295|lt=Kamō Town|1=Kamō, Kagoshima}}
| 地図 = 
| 緯度度 = 
| 経度度 = 
}}

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=kusunoki|1=Camphora officinarum}} tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|en|Aira, Kagoshima|WD=Q407947|lt=Aira City|1=Aira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>{{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 23 ({{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}})). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ({{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}}) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

== History ==
According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Waki Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the {{ill|Hōan|en|Hōan|WD=Q1194464|lt=Hōan era|1=Hōan}} (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ({{ill|Shinboku|en|Shinboku|WD=Q48743901|lt=Shinboku|1=Shinboku}}), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Eishō (Muromachi period)|en|Eishō (Muromachi period)|WD=Q1193577|lt=Eishō Era|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ({{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1|1=1913}}), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument|1=Natural monument}}, and later, on March 29, 1952 ({{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27|1=1952}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in {{ill|Izumi, Kagoshima|en|Izumi, Kagoshima|WD=Q15734|lt=Izumi City|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* Kamō Town Local History Magazine Committee, ''Kamō Town Local History'', Kamō Town, 1991

== External Links ==
{{Commonscat}}
* {{National Cultural Property Database|401|2978|Kamō no Kus}}
* [https://www.kagoshima-kankou.com/guide/11083 "Kamō no Ōkus – Japan’s Largest Giant Tree"] - {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture Tourism Federation|ja|鹿児島県観光連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefecture Tourism Federation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
* [https://www.jataff.jp/monument/48.html "Kamō no Kus"] - {{ill|Japan Agricultural, Forestry and Fisheries Technology Promotion Association|ja|農林水産・食品産業技術振興協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Agricultural, Forestry and Fisheries Technology Promotion Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579675|1=Japan Association for Techno-innovation in Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries}}

It is further recorded that by the 4th year of the {{ill|Hōan|en|Hōan|WD=Q1194464|lt=Hōan era|1=Hōan}} (1123), when {{ill|Kamō Shunsei|ja|蒲生舜清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Shunsei|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} founded Kamō Hachiman Shrine, the tree was already venerated as a sacred tree ({{ill|Shinboku|en|Shinboku|WD=Q48743901|lt=Shinboku|1=Shinboku}}), suggesting that it was already of considerable size at that time.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社">[http://www.kamou80000.com/okusu.html {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} “Japan’s Largest Giant Tree”] (accessed October 16, 2021)</ref> In the 3rd year of the {{ill|Eishō (Muromachi period)|en|Eishō (Muromachi period)|WD=Q1193577|lt=Eishō Era|1=Eishō (Muromachi period)}} (1506), a fire broke out on January 7 in the forest of Hachiman, resulting in the burning of some branches.

In 1913 ({{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1|1=1913}}), the tree was featured in the "Great Japanese Old Trees Ranking" as the Eastern Yokozuna, and on March 8 of Taishō 11 it was designated a {{ill|Natural monument|en|Natural monument|WD=Q23790|lt=Natural Monument|1=Natural monument}}, and later, on March 29, 1952 ({{ill|1952|en|1952|WD=Q5272|lt=Showa 27|1=1952}}), it was designated as a {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Natural monument|WD=Q23790}}. On August 31, 1985 (Showa 60), due to strong winds from {{ill|Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja|昭和60th year 台風第13号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Typhoon No. 13 of Showa 60|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, many branches and leaves were lost; however, through the protective efforts of local residents, the tree has recovered.

It is noted that another tree, the "Great Kus" of Izumi in {{ill|Izumi, Kagoshima|en|Izumi, Kagoshima|WD=Q15734|lt=Izumi City|1=Izumi, Kagoshima}}, which is designated as a municipal natural monument, is considered a kindred tree to Kamō no Kus, and a tragic love legend is associated with the two.<ref name="蒲生八幡神社"/>

'''Kamō no Kus''' (蒲生のクス, かもうのクス) is a giant {{ill|Camphora officinarum|en|Camphora officinarum|WD=Q158722|lt=kusunoki|1=Camphora officinarum}} tree located within the precincts of {{ill|Kamō Hachiman Shrine|ja|蒲生八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamō Town, upper Hisayutaka, in {{ill|Aira, Kagoshima|en|Aira, Kagoshima|WD=Q407947|lt=Aira City|1=Aira, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. It has been designated as a National {{ill|Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=Natural monument|WD=Q23790}}.

== Overview ==
{{Location map+|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|relief=1|width=240|float=right|caption=Location of {{ill|Kamō no Kus|ja|蒲生のクス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamō no Kus|1=Kamō no Kusu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621332}}|places=
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.765764|long=130.569442|position=left|label='''Kamō no Kus'''|mark=Red pog.svg}}
{{Location map~|Japan Kagoshima Prefecture|lat=31.596806|long=130.557139|position=left|label=<small>{{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}</small>|mark=Green pog.svg}}
}}
The tree stands approximately 30 meters tall, with a trunk circumference of 24.2 meters measured at a height of 1.3 meters above ground level, and a root spread of 33.5 meters. An internal cavity measuring about 13 square meters (equivalent to eight tatami mats) has been detected within the trunk. Its age is estimated at around 1500 years (as of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 23 ({{ill|2011|en|2011|WD=Q1994|lt=2011|1=2011}})). A survey conducted by the {{ill|Environmental Agency|ja|環境庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Environmental Agency|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 環境省|1=Ministry of the Environment (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1125558}} in Showa 63 ({{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}}) recognized it as the largest giant tree in Japan. It is also sometimes referred to as "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大楠) or "Kamō no Ōkus" (蒲生の大クス).

According to tradition, during the {{ill|Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|ja|宇佐八幡宮神託事件|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Hachiman Shrine Oracle Incident|1=Dōkyō Incident|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449036}}, {{ill|Wake no Kiyomaro|en|Wake no Kiyomaro|WD=Q7961005|lt=Waki Kiyomaro|1=Wake no Kiyomaro}}—exiled to Ōsumi—visited the area, and it is said that the staff (杖) he carried was driven into the ground and took root (a legend known as the {{ill|Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja|杖立て伝説|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsue Tate Densetsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}).
